DECLARATION OF SARAH R LUSTIG IN OPPOS. TO PLTF'S MTN FOR SUMMARY AJUDICATION AS TO DEFENSES OF DEFT'S; MTN TO BIFURICATE LIABILITY AND DAMAGES FILED BY DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE, A BUSINESS ENTITY OF UNKNOWN FORM MCGARTY, ERIC AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY MENTO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. MOTION UNTIMELY SINCE DEFENDANT LOYALTY LABS GENERAL APPEARANCE WAS AUGUST 3, 2009. [CCP SECTION 437C(A).] MOTION DOES NOT ADDRESS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AND DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF ANY CAUSES OF ACTION. [CCP SECTION 437C(F)(1).] SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT SEPARATELY IDENTIFY EACH CLAIM, CAUSE OF ACTION OR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. (CRC 3.1350) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER MCGARTY REGARDED PLAINTIFF'S COMMENTS AS A COMPLAINT. (MCGARTY DEPO, 40:18-20) PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT SHOW THAT CO-DEFENDANT MEDINA ACTUALLY HARASSED PLAINTIFF AND HE DOES NOT DENY THAT HE HARASSED MEDINA. CO-DEFENDANT MEDINA HAS PROVIDED A DECLARATION THAT DISPUTES PLAINTIFF'S VERSION OF THE EVENTS. PLAINTIFF FIRST COMPLAINED OF HARASSMENT ON 4/4/08 BUT DID NOT WORK FOR UPS AFTER THAT DATE WHICH MOOTS FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE AND CORRECTIVE ACTION BECAUSE THERE WAS NO NEW HARASSMENT. ALSO, COURT RULES ON DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS AS FOLLOWS: 1-8; 26-31; 34-36 ARE SUSTAINED; 19; 21-25; 32; 33; 35; 37; 38 40-45 ARE OVERRULED. MOTION TO BIFURCATE DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO TRIAL COURT'S DETERMINATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (ORDER RE: OBJECTIONS ALSO SIGNED.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice And Motion To Quash Subpoena For Pltf Mento'S Employment Records
Off Calendar Jan-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Arcolina Panto, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: ANTHONY MENTO
Sara R. Lustig, Esq., appearing by telephone; for Defendant and opposing party: UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted AS MODIFIED.
PLAINTIFF ANTHONY MENTO's Motion To Quash Subpoena For Plaintiff's Employment Records is granted as the subpoena is overly broad and invades Plaintiff's right to privacy without good cause. The court finds that the subpoena was a misuse of the discovery process. The court finds that the opposition was made without substantial justification.
MODIFICATION: AWARD FOR Sanctions IS STRIKEN. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewllett. Not reported.

The Revelation Law Firm (510) 665-4195 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Laura C. McHugh, of RM&H, (916) 442-0034 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Dec-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ANTHONY MENTO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Melanie D. Popper, Esq. & Arcolina Panto, Esq. of the Revelation Law Firm (510) 665-4195 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Alden Parker, Esq. of Basham Parker LLP (916) 925-5850 appearing for Defendants Medina; and Loyalty Labs, Inc.; Sarah R. Lustig, Esq. & Laura C. McHugh, Esq. of RM&H, LLP (916) 442-0034 appearing for Defendant Universal Protection SVC, and opposing party. (Plaintiff is not contesting tentative ruling as to portion regarding Loyalty Labs.) Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. MOTION UNTIMELY SINCE DEFENDANT LOYALTY LABS GENERAL APPEARANCE WAS AUGUST 3, 2009. [CCP SECTION 437c(a).] MOTION DOES NOT ADDRESS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AND DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF ANY CAUSES OF ACTION. [CCP SECTION 437c(f)(1).] SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT SEPARATELY IDENTIFY EACH CLAIM, CAUSE OF ACTION OR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. (CRC 3.1350) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER MCGARTY REGARDED PLAINTIFF'S COMMENTS AS A COMPLAINT. (MCGARTY DEPO, 40:18-20) PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT SHOW THAT CO-DEFENDANT MEDINA ACTUALLY HARASSED PLAINTIFF AND HE DOES NOT DENY THAT HE HARASSED MEDINA. CO-DEFENDANT MEDINA HAS PROVIDED A DECLARATION THAT DISPUTES PLAINTIFF'S VERSION OF THE EVENTS. PLAINTIFF FIRST COMPLAINED OF HARASSMENT ON 4/4/08 BUT DID NOT WORK FOR UPS AFTER THAT DATE WHICH MOOTS FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE AND CORRECTIVE ACTION BECAUSE THERE WAS NO NEW HARASSMENT. Also, Court rules on Defendants objections as follows: 1-8; 26-31; 34-36 are sustained; 19; 21-25; 32; 33; 35; 37; 38 40-45 are overruled. MOTION TO BIFURCATE DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO TRIAL COURT'S DETERMINATION. Order signed in open Court. (Order re: objections also signed.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Oct-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Sarah R. Lustig, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Universal Protection Service and Eric McGarty
Arcolina Panto, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Anthony Mento

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Universal Protection Service Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions
Motion to compel further response to form interrogatories and for monetary sanctions granted in part, denied in part:
1. As to form interrogatory no. 6.5, granted; plaintiff shall serve a verified supplemental response that responds with specific reference to each condition listed in response to form interrogatory no. 6.2

2. As to form interrogatory no. 6.7, granted; plaintiff shall serve a verified supplemental response that responds specifically to this interrogatory.

3. As to form interrogatory no. 17.1, as it relates to plaintiff's response to request for admission nos. 10, 11, 13, 14, and 44, granted; plaintiff shall serve a verified supplemental response

4. Request for sanctions denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
